timeline.label: Dell GPU purchase ~$1.6B to deploy at Childress for previously announced $3.4B NVIDIA managed-services AI cloud contract
Additional Dell GPU purchase agreements (US and Canada) to support AI cloud build-out
Microsoft GPU infrastructure contract ~$9.7B through 2031 (Horizon 1-4, ~200 MW, NVIDIA GB300) plus ~$5.8B Dell GPU supply agreement
Nine-month FY2026 revenue $569.8M (+82% YoY); cash $2.21B; convertible debt proceeds $3.3B; impairment $188M
FY2025 revenue $484.6M (+163% YoY); net income $86.9M; operating cash flow $245.9M; ~40.1 EH/s and ~650 MW capacity
U.S. domestic issuer reporting transition (20-F to 10-K/10-Q cadence)
FY2024 revenue $184.1M; continued expansion at Childress and BC sites
Initial NVIDIA H100 GPU purchase (~248 units, ~$10M) for early HPC/AI services
Nasdaq listing via SPAC merger (Iris Energy Limited)
Company founded; renewable-powered bitcoin mining at scale in Australia and North America

industry.structure: Bitcoin mining is commoditized hash-rate competition with power cost as the primary margin driver; AI GPU hosting is oligopolistic with NVIDIA supply constraints and hyperscaler buyers holding pricing power.
industry.share_shift: IREN is pivoting from pure-play mining peer set (MARA, RIOT, CLSK) toward AI infrastructure landlords (APLD, CRWV); early-mover advantage on energized Texas power sites versus greenfield interconnection queues.
industry.trend: Miners re-rate on AI contract headlines while funding multi-billion GPU capex via converts; execution risk on Dell deliveries and Microsoft acceptance testing is the near-term bottleneck.
